It is a great honour to have been elected as the Conservative Member of Parliament for South Shropshire. I am committed to listening to your concerns, supporting our local community and delivering the services you deserve. Together, we can ensure that South Shropshire remains a fantastic place to live and work. Read my story here Health & Social Care Survey I have launched my Health and Social Care campaign. Please take a few minutes to fill in my survey so I can find the best solutions for health and social care in South Shropshire. pages.title: About Me | Stuart Anderson MP
pages.meta_description: It is a great honour to have been elected as the Conservative Member of Parliament for South Shropshire. I have spent over 40 years of my life based in the Marches, growing up in Herefordshire and now living in Shropshire with my wife and children.
headings: h2: Stuart Anderson Celebrates First Year Anniversary as South Shropshire's MP
h2: Stuart Anderson MP Re-Adopted as South Shropshire's Conservative Candidate
h3: Stuart Anderson MP South Shropshire
pages.text_excerpt: About Me | Stuart Anderson MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Me South Shropshire Parliament News Contact About Me Tweet It is a great honour to have been elected as the Conservative Member of Parliament for South Shropshire. I have spent over 40 years of my life based in the Marches, growing up in Herefordshire and now living in Shropshire with my wife and children. It is a great joy for me and my family to be living in South Shropshire, for my children to be part of the local schools and to grow up enjoying the beautiful countryside around us. I have spent over 40 years of my life based in the Marches, growing up in Herefordshire and now living in Shropshire with my wife and children My personal journey has simple beginnings, as a farm hand in rural Herefordshire. At the age of sixteen, motivated by my own father’s career in the SAS, I made the decision to serve our country by joining the Army. At the age of 17, I suffered a high velocity gunshot wound to the foot during a live firing attack, shattering my foot. I was advised that my foot would require amputation, and that I would never walk again without a walking stick. My military career was apparently over. However after 10 months of gruelling rehabilitation, I returned to full active duty to serve on multiple operational tours. After 8 years in the Royal Green Jackets, now the Rifles (the local Shropshire regiment), I went into the defence and security industry, obtaining a MSc in Security and Risk Management and operating extensively around the globe. I believe that my experiences from my time in defence and security provided me with a real-world grounding that I can apply practically to supporting local communities. I have spoken openly about the impact of getting shot at a young age on my mental health. After hitting rock bottom, I was (against all odds) able to rebuild my life. Having been given what I felt was a second chance I wanted to use my life experience to make a difference. My passion to continue to serve my country, the same passion that inspired me to become a soldier, remains. Through politics, I believe that I am able to serve in a different way and bring positive change to the everyday lives of hard-working people. This is why I entered politics and have served in Parliament since 2019. My passion to continue to serve my country, the same passion that inspired me to become a soldier, remains The people of South Shropshire deserve strong representation that can secure good public services, solve local issues and secure funding and support for business and the farming community. As your MP, I am committed to working hard to deliver for the residents of South Shropshire. This means I will always listen to your concerns, support our local community and deliver the funding, support and services you deserve. It is a privilege to serve as MP for an area that means so much to me and my family. News Stuart Anderson Celebrates First Year Anniversary as South Shropshire's MP Thursday, 3 July, 2025 Stuart Anderson MP has marked his first anniversary since being elected to represent the people of South Shropshire in Parliament. Stuart Anderson MP Re-Adopted as South Shropshire's Conservative Candidate Tuesday, 3 June, 2025 Stuart Anderson MP Re-Adopted as South Shropshire's Conservative CandidateStuart Anderson MP has been re-adopted by South Shropshire Conservative Association just ten months into his first term in office. National Highways gave assurances in March 2026 that works would be completed by the end of April 2026. Stuart has now requested an urgent meeting with National Highways to secure a clear explanation. pages.meta_description: Stuart Anderson MP has launched a major review into the delivery of health and social care services in South Shropshire.
headings: h2: Stuart Anderson MP Marks World Cancer Day 2026
h2: Stuart Anderson MP Launches Health and Social Care Campaign
h3: Stuart Anderson MP South Shropshire
pages.text_excerpt: SURVEY - Health & Social Care | Stuart Anderson MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Me South Shropshire Parliament News Contact SURVEY - Health & Social Care Tweet Stuart Anderson MP has launched a major review into the delivery of health and social care services in South Shropshire. As part of this, Stuart is inviting residents to respond to a new survey which seeks information on their experience of using local health and social care services. It is part of Stuart’s long term campaign to enhance public services in South Shropshire. Stuart wants understand the pressures they are under and support efforts to improve services like community hospitals. Stuart has said that he is determined to reduce rural disparities in the access and quality of these services to achieve better outcomes. Health & Social Care 2 Current Your views National Issues Get involved Your details Leave this field blank How satisfied are you with local NHS primary care services (GPs, dentists, pharmacies etc.)? (Select up to 3) GP access Dental access Ambulance response times Hospital waiting times Mental health support Social care availability Care for older people Services for children and families Transport to appointments Is there anything else you would like to tell us about health or social care in your area? News View From Westminster: February 2026 Thursday, 12 February, 2026 On Wednesday 4th February, I chaired a Westminster Hall Debate in Parliament on the state of postal services in rural areas. I was delighted to secure this debate, because I have seen over the last few months just how severely my constituents have been impacted by utterly unacceptable delays. pages.text_excerpt: Advice Surgeries | Stuart Anderson MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Me South Shropshire Parliament News Contact Advice Surgeries Tweet Advice surgeries are for personal matters which you need to raise with Stuart. You must book an appointment before attending a surgery. You can register your interest using the link below and the office will contact you to arrange an appointment. Upcoming dates: 22nd May - Bridgnorth 5th June - Condover 3rd July - Chirbury 24th July - Bridgnorth Register your interest Please note: Stuart's advice surgeries are often booked up far in advance. Please do not consider yourself to have an appointment booked until this has been confirmed by Stuart's office, who will provide you with a precise time and location.
